headline: Stock Futures Fall as Investor Weigh Up Big Tech Earnings, Iran Fears
summary: Stocks looked set to edge higher on Thursday as investors tried to make sense of a deluge of Big Tech earnings and a sudden jump in oil prices.  S&P 500 futures climbed 0.1% and contracts tied to the tech-heavy Nasdaq 100 added 0.2%.  The three major indexes struggled for direction on Wednesday after the Federal Reserve kept interest rates unchanged, as was widely expected.

content.title: Stock Futures Fall as Investor Weigh Up Big Tech Earnings, Iran Fears
content.summary: Stocks looked set to edge higher on Thursday as investors tried to make sense of a deluge of Big Tech earnings and a sudden jump in oil prices.  S&P 500 futures climbed 0.1% and contracts tied to the tech-heavy Nasdaq 100 added 0.2%.  The three major indexes struggled for direction on Wednesday after the Federal Reserve kept interest rates unchanged, as was widely expected.

headline: Google Cloud pulls ahead as Big Tech's AI bet swells to $700 billion

headline: Amazon Stock Bounces Back Following Positive Chip Demand Comments
summary: Amazon said that revenue from its Amazon Web Services cloud unit rose 28% from the prior year to $37.6 billion. CEO Andy Jassy noted in the earnings release that it was AWS’s fastest growth in 15 quarters.

headline: Fed Inflation Gauge Hits 2-Year High As AI Boosts GDP; S&P 500 Rises (Live Coverage)
summary: The Federal Reserve's primary inflation measure hit its highest level since November 2023, while first-quarter GDP growth slightly undershot forecasts, despite a huge boost in spending on AI-related equipment and software.  The S&P 500 rose modestly after the data as investors weighed earnings reports and capital spending plans from Google, Amazon.com, Meta Platforms and Microsoft.  The inflation data follows Wednesday's hawkish policy signals at the final Fed meeting presided over by Chairman Jerome Powell.

content.title: Fed Inflation Gauge Hits 2-Year High As AI Boosts GDP; S&P 500 Rises (Live Coverage)
content.summary: The Federal Reserve's primary inflation measure hit its highest level since November 2023, while first-quarter GDP growth slightly undershot forecasts, despite a huge boost in spending on AI-related equipment and software.  The S&P 500 rose modestly after the data as investors weighed earnings reports and capital spending plans from Google, Amazon.com, Meta Platforms and Microsoft.  The inflation data follows Wednesday's hawkish policy signals at the final Fed meeting presided over by Chairman Jerome Powell.

headline: Oil prices whipsaw while US stocks glide near their record heights
summary: Oil prices whipsawed on Thursday and surged toward their highest levels since the war with Iran began, only for the leaps to quickly vanish.  The U.S. stock market, meanwhile, is gliding following more strong profit reports from big companies like Alphabet and Amazon.  The S&P 500 rose 0.2% and inched closer to its all-time high set earlier this week, as companies continue to deliver stronger profits for the start of 2026 than analysts expected despite high oil prices and uncertainty about the economy.

content.title: Oil prices whipsaw while US stocks glide near their record heights

The U.S. stock market, meanwhile, is gliding following more strong profit reports from big companies like Alphabet and Amazon.  The S&P 500 rose 0.2% and inched closer to its all-time high set earlier this week, as companies continue to deliver stronger profits for the start of 2026 than analysts expected despite high oil prices and uncertainty about the economy.

headline: BREAKINGVIEWS-Meta's fall shows punters crave clearer AI payoff
summary: NEW YORK, April 30 (Reuters Breakingviews) - Shares fell 10% despite strong ‌revenue growth and signs technology is boosting ‌ad sales. The issue is funding: unlike Alphabet, Amazon ​or Microsoft, there's no cloud arm to cushion infrastructure spending. A rapidly rising bet with an unclear, slow payoff makes investors queasy.

headline: Fed Inflation Gauge Hits 2-Year High As AI Boosts GDP; S&P 500 Rises
summary: The Federal Reserve's primary inflation measure hit its highest level since November 2023, while first-quarter GDP growth slightly undershot forecasts, despite a huge boost in spending on AI-related equipment and software.  The S&P 500 rose modestly after the data as investors weighed earnings reports and capital spending plans from Google, Amazon.com, Meta Platforms and Microsoft.  The inflation data follows Wednesday's hawkish policy signals at the final Fed meeting presided over by Chairman Jerome Powell.

content.title: Fed Inflation Gauge Hits 2-Year High As AI Boosts GDP; S&P 500 Rises
content.summary: The Federal Reserve's primary inflation measure hit its highest level since November 2023, while first-quarter GDP growth slightly undershot forecasts, despite a huge boost in spending on AI-related equipment and software.  The S&P 500 rose modestly after the data as investors weighed earnings reports and capital spending plans from Google, Amazon.com, Meta Platforms and Microsoft.  The inflation data follows Wednesday's hawkish policy signals at the final Fed meeting presided over by Chairman Jerome Powell.

headline: Big Tech Spending Is Back in Focus. How Apple Stands Out.
summary: Wednesday night was a busy one for the stock market as Microsoft, Meta Platforms, Amazon.com, and Alphabet all reported their most recent quarterly results.  One key takeaway: These massive cloud providers have no plans to slow down their hefty investments towards building out the infrastructure needed to power AI.  Microsoft said it expects to invest about $190 billion in capex for the year, which was well ahead of Wall Street’s 2026 estimate of $160 billion.

content.title: Big Tech Spending Is Back in Focus. How Apple Stands Out.

One key takeaway: These massive cloud providers have no plans to slow down their hefty investments towards building out the infrastructure needed to power AI.  Microsoft said it expects to invest about $190 billion in capex for the year, which was well ahead of Wall Street’s 2026 estimate of $160 billion.

headline: Amazon Q1 Revenues Soar Y/Y, AWS Growth Hits 15-Quarter High
summary: AMZN's Q1 sales jump 17% to $181.5B as AWS posts fastest growth in 15 quarters, powering record margins and strong profit surge.
